mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.25%, the Federal Reserve maintains a restrictive monetary stance that keeps real rates elevated. This suppresses equity valuations and increases Treasury yields as investors demand higher compensation for duration risk. The mechanism operates continuously in tightening regimes.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines in more than 55% of 20-day periods when FEDFUNDS > 4.25%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above 4.5% signals labor market deterioration and recession risk, triggering a flight-to-safety bid in Treasuries that compresses yields downward. This mechanism activates when unemployment crosses the threshold and persists for at least 2 months.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in more than 50% of 20-day periods following UNRATE > 4.5% threshold breach, or signal fails to produce negative average returns in triggered regimes (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly signal aggregate demand strength. When both series decline month-over-month simultaneously, it indicates demand destruction that reduces inflation expectations and Treasury yields. The interaction captures non-linear demand shock effects.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline MoM
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in more than 52% of 20-day periods when both RSAFS and HOUST decline MoM, or Rank IC of interaction term < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth above 104.0 indicates robust supply-side expansion that sustains productivity gains and moderates inflation. This supports higher real yields and nominal Treasury yields as growth expectations strengthen without inflation acceleration.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines in more than 55% of 60-day periods when INDPRO > 104.0, or Rank IC < 0.06 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS interact to determine real rate expectations. When inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 3.2% while FEDFUNDS remains below 4.5%, the Fed is behind the curve, forcing future rate hikes that raise Treasury yields. The interaction captures policy lag effects.
regime_hint: monetary policy lag with inflation persistence
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines in more than 50% of 20-day periods when CPIAUCSL > 3.2% and FEDFUNDS < 4.5%, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: PAYEMS growth below 100K per month signals labor market weakness and reduced wage pressure, lowering inflation expectations and Treasury yields. This always-on mechanism reflects the continuous relationship between employment growth and inflation dynamics.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in more than 55% of 5-day periods when PAYEMS growth is below 100K MoM, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly determine demand-driven inflation. When both series show quarter-over-quarter acceleration above trend, it signals demand-pull inflation that forces the Fed to maintain higher rates, raising Treasury yields.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ growth > 2.5% and RSAFS MoM growth > 0.5%
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines in more than 52% of 60-day periods when both GDPC1 QoQ > 2.5% and RSAFS MoM > 0.5%, or interaction term Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO declines below 103.0 while UNRATE remains below 4.2%, it signals stagflationary pressure: supply constraints without labor slack. This regime forces the Fed to prioritize inflation control, raising Treasury yields despite weak growth.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines in more than 55% of 20-day periods when INDPRO < 103.0 and UNRATE < 4.2%, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in any 2-year rolling window.
